title: lilium puberulum
intro: Lilium puberulum, also known as the hairy lily, is a species of lily native to the Himalayas. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The hairy lily has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
